Data/Tutorial/DT_TutorialMessages.uexp: Welcome {Employee}! Use WASD or <Key id="Gamepad_Left2D"/>  to move around, move the mouse cursor to look around and press [{Key0}] to interact. We will explain things as we go.

Pay attention to the doppler radar! It warns you about imminent tornados and storms. It can (hopefully) help you avoid them. It also tells you where other employees are and whether they are alive, or not...
The monitors let you see what other employees are doing, and help you work better as a team (if you see anyone slacking off please report it).

Up to for doing some extra hours? No? Too bad, they are mandatory. The mission board lists secondary objectives other than escaping. Completing them will grant extra rewards (and help you keep your job).
Communication is key. To use the walkie-talkie press [{Key0}] to turn it on and off and [{Key1}] to talk to your teammates. Please limit conversation to work-related topics, such as your survival.
This is the storage box. You have to bring all collectables you find here, newspapers, VHS tapes, files, and the like, otherwise you will not get any rewards out of them, company policy.
Can we fix it? Probably, you better hope so. Toolboxes can be used to remove both broken tires and batteries on the van. To use it, press [{Key0}] while highlighting them.
Scissor Jacks can be used to lift up the van and change broken tires. To use it, press [{Key0}] while highlighting a flat tire.

Defibrillators can bring a fellow employee back from the brink of death!, it can also let them keep working for us. To use it, hold [{Key0}] to charge it up, and aim at a downed employee to revive them.
Umbrellas protect you and other nearby employees from getting wet by rain, and damaged by hail or acid rain when held. Just make sure to put it away if there is a lightning storm.
This is a consumable. Like the name suggests, it will get consumed after use. Hold [{Key0}] to use it, each one either heals you or gives different temporary benefits.

These are the fuses, they protect the entire electricity network of the van. If they break, the lights on the van will flicker. Press and hold [{Key0}] to remove the blown out fuses, then get new fuses to replace them.
This is the oil reservoir. Press and hold the [{Key0}] to open and close it, and [{Key1}] to fill it up when you have an oil bottle.
This is the coolant reservoir. Press and hold [{Key0}] to open and close it, and [{Key1}] to fill it up when you have a coolant bottle.
This is the fuel tank. Press and hold [{Key0}] to open and close it, and [{Key1}] to fill it up when you have a fuel can.
This is a fuel can, you should bring it to the fuel tank on the left side of the van and press [{Key0}] to fill it up.
This is an oil bottle, you should bring it to the oil reservoir on the front of the van and press [{Key0}] to fill it up.
This is a coolant bottle, you should bring it to the coolant reservoir on the front of the van and press [{Key0}] to fill it up.

This is a new tire, you can replace any broken tire on the van with this one. First you need a scissors jack to lift up the van and a toolbox to remove the old tire. Once you do that, just press [{Key0}] to install the new one.
These are new fuses, you can replace any blown out fuses that where removed by pressing [{Key0}].
This is a new battery, you can replace a broken battery that was removed with a toolbox by pressing [{Key0}].
A collectable! Take it back to the collectables box on the van and you will get a bonus for your trouble (only if you survive, otherwise it will NOT go to your next of kin).

Rain will simply get you wet while you are not under a roof, nothing too serious on its own, but be careful with electricity while wet! You should get an umbrella, please be a team player and share it with other employees.
Acid rain will damage you constantly while you are not under a roof. Should you get an umbrella, please be a team player and share it with other employees.
Hail has a chance of damaging you while you are not under a roof. Should you get an umbrella, please be a team player and share it with other employees.

Pay attention employee, certain dangerous actions will require you to be very precise. You must press [{Key0}] when the indicator is on the green area to successfully perform the action. If you fail, you might take damage. Some interactions will require multiple presses to be completed.
Where your survival begins! The workbench will spawn useful tools for the match. Some are essential to fix the van, others help you navigate the map safely! Use them wisely, time is money!
Communication is key to teamwork! This whiteboard is great tool for that, write on it and coordinate survival plans. Keep all drawings and messages strictly professional and work-related.
Information is authorized on a need-to-know basis. This company computer houses the official archives, as you evolve as an employee and complete missions, you will unlock more restricted archives.

Dressing appropriately is company policy! Use the locker to change your uniform, which will alter certain aspects of your gameplay. You can also review your hard-earned official merits here. Look sharp, employee!
Welcome to the APEX internal broadcast system! Grab the radio microphone and press [{Key0}] to transmit your voice to your teammates.
Data/Loading/DT_LoadingScreenTips.uexp: Cleaning the van, please stand by
Refilling blinker fluid, please stand by
Watch your step! Open manholes are a hazard
Caught in a tornado? Crouch to resist it
Carrying heavy items reduces movement speed
Avoid live wires, it is a shocking experience
Lost? Start the van to see your next objective
Use umbrellas to shield nearby crewmates
Use portable conductors to protect crew
Keep your distance from burning crewmates
Recover APEX property to maximize your rating
Complete extra tasks to boost your rating
Wait your turn on the Walkie-Talkie
Data/Achievements/DT_Achievements.uexp: Get caught up by a tornado while holding an umbrella.
Hit a bulls eye on the dart board in the van.
Shock your friends with the defibrillator.
Avoid lightning damage by holding the portable conductor when hit.
Change 4 flat tires in the van.
Be revived after passing out in game.
Fail at a repair minigame for the first time.
Fail at a repair minigame 15 times.
Succeed at a repair minigame for the first time.
Drink 5 energy drinks in a minute.
Win without ever leaving the van.
Get caught up by a tornado for the first time.
Get damaged by fire for the first time.
Get hit by lightning for the first time.
Have 10 darts on you at the same time.
Get on top of the van for the first time.
Escape the end tornado for the first time.
Hit other players with darts 50 times.
Fail to escape the city for the first time.
Collect 50 items found in the city.
Collect 100 items found in the city.
Collect 500 items found in the city.
Buy something from the vending machine for the first time.
Spend 100 tokens in the vending machine.
Spend 500 tokens in the vending machine.
Spend 1000 tokens in the vending machine.

Data/Settings/SettingsData.uexp: Controls how loud the game is overall.
Controls how loud the music is.
Controls how loud gameplay sounds are.
Controls how loud weather sound effects are.
Controls how loud menu and UI sounds are.
Specifically controls the volume of tornadoes during gameplay
Controls how loud other players sound.
When enabled, aligns the original and the reverse audio stream to negate the output inside the input, resulting in effective echo cancellation.
When enabled, the high-pass filter will remove low-frequency content from the input audio signal, thus making it sound cleaner and more focused.

When enabled, the noise suppressor will remove distracting background noise from the input audio signal. You can control the aggressiveness of the suppression. Increasing the level will reduce the noise level at the expense of a higher speech distortion.
When enabled, the transient suppressor will try to detect and attenuate keyboard clicks.
When enabled, the gain controller will automatically bring the signal to an appropriate range. This means input signals with low volume will be amplified and high volume will be limited.
Selects which input device (microphone) the game uses.
Controls how loud your microphone is to other players.
Selects which output device (speakers/headphones) the game uses.
Controls how the game is displayed on your screen.
Uses the entire screen for best performance.
Fills the screen and allows fast switching to other apps.
Runs the game in a resizable window.
Controls the game's display resolution. Higher values produce sharper visuals but may impact performance.
Limits how many frames per second the game can render. When VSync is enabled, this setting may not apply.
NVIDIA Reflex Low Latency reduces PC latency and increases responsiveness.
AMD FidelityFX Super Resolution Frame Generation increases frame rate by creating additional frames computed from existing inputs.
NVIDIA DLSS Frame Generation increases frame rate by creating additional frames computed from existing inputs.
Controls Lumen Global Illumination. When enabled, ray-traced lighting allows light to bounce between surfaces for more realistic results. Has a significant performance impact.
Enables or disables Lumen reflections. When enabled, ray-traced reflections improve accuracy on surfaces like water and shiny materials. Has a significant performance impact.
Selects the technique used to smooth jagged edges in the image. Each method offers a different balance between image quality and performance.
Controls the quality level of the selected anti-aliasing method. Higher values improve image clarity at a performance cost.
Controls AMD FidelityFX Super Resolution upscaling quality. Higher values improve image clarity at a performance cost.
Controls NVIDIA DLSS upscaling quality. Higher values improve image clarity at a performance cost.

Controls the resolution the game is rendered at before being scaled to your screen. Higher values produce a sharper image but reduce performance. Lower values improve performance but may look softer or blurrier.
Applies a preset that adjusts multiple graphics settings at once.
(* This is an experimental feature. GPU issues and lower performance are expected)
Controls how far objects and scenery are visible. Higher values increase detail at long distances.
Controls the quality of Lumen Global Illumination. Higher settings improve the accuracy and stability of indirect lighting but may reduce performance.
Controls the quality of post processing effects including ambient occlusion, bloom, motion blur, depth of field, and color grading. Higher values increase the quality of these effects.
Controls the quality and resolution of shadows. Higher values produce sharper, more accurate shadows.
Controls the quality of reflections on surfaces like water and shiny materials. Higher values improve reflection detail and accuracy.
Controls the resolution of textures used in the game. Higher values improve surface detail but require more video memory.
Controls the quality of visual effects including particles, translucency, refraction, and screen-space reflections. Higher values increase the quality and accuracy of these effects.
Controls the density of grass, bushes, and other foliage.
Controls subtle material shading features. Low visual and performance impact.
Controls the quality of volumetric fog. Higher values improve fog detail and smoothness but may reduce performance.
Controls the quality of bloom (glow around bright lights). Higher values produce smoother, more natural bloom but may reduce performance.
Reverses the direction of the scroll for the Inventory.
Reverses up and down look controls.
Reverses left and right look controls.
Controls how much the camera moves up and down when moving the mouse.
Controls how much the camera moves left and right when moving the mouse.
Adjusts the overall size of the user interface.
Adjusts the strength of the CRT post-processing effect.
Controls which measurement units the game uses.
Whether to show tutorials throughout the game.
Show/Hide the HUD Tokens Added animation
Controls the camera's field of view. Higher values may impact performance.
Controls how strong motion blur appears during camera movement.
Adjusts the overall brightness of the image by offsetting the camera exposure. Higher values make the image brighter, while lower values make it darker.
Controls the strength of camera shake from movement actions. Lower values reduce or disable shake.
Controls the strength of camera shake when taking damage. Lower values reduce or disable shake.
Controls the strength of camera shake when interacting with items or objects. Lower values reduce or disable shake.
If true, voice chat code can run in the editor. Restart required.
Data/Entitlements/VendingItems/DA_Bandag: Might not be a medkit but it will do.
Data/Entitlements/VendingItems/DA_Crowba: Forcing entry has never been easier, just watch where you swing that thing.
<General>Opens locked doors and deals melee damage to players</>.
Data/Entitlements/VendingItems/DA_Defibr: Clear! Just make sure they are actually dead before you apply the pads.
<General>Revives downed teammates or knocks out conscious players</>.
Data/Entitlements/VendingItems/DA_Flashl: Ideal for the darkest environments, so nobody misses any details.
Data/Entitlements/VendingItems/DA_Granol: A quick snack since every second counts.
Data/Entitlements/VendingItems/DA_Medkit: A premium medkit, to save you from any situation on the field.
Data/Entitlements/VendingItems/DA_Portab: Grounds high-voltage threats instantly. Side effects of direct contact may include extreme regret.
<General>Deflects lightning strikes away from nearby allies, has a chance of breaking upon impact</>.
<General>Scans the local area for weather anomalies and items</>.
Data/Entitlements/VendingItems/DA_Umbrel: A heavy-duty shield against the elements, though it makes for a terrible lightning rod.
<General>Protects nearby players from rain, hail, and acid storms</>.
Data/Moodlets/DA_Anchor.uexp: Represents the player being anchored, this gives the player resistance to getting dragged by tornadoes
Data/Moodlets/DA_Drenched.uexp: Represents the player when they standed unprotected in the rain for more than 20 seconds. Will go away after 60 seconds of not being in contact the rain. (having contact with the rain will restart the timer)
Data/Moodlets/DA_FireProof.uexp: Represents the player having damage reducction on fire damage
Data/Moodlets/DA_Hail.uexp: A hail storm is damaging you.
Data/Moodlets/DA_LightningProtection.uex: Represents if the player is protected against lightning strikes
Data/Moodlets/DA_Overemcumbered.uexp: Represents the player when they are carrying a heavy/big object. Will only go await after the player drops the heavy item.
Data/Moodlets/DA_RainProtection.uexp: Represents if a player is inside the protection area of an Iron Umbrella. It will make the player invulnerable to acid rain and hail storms

This game depicts natural disasters and their aftermath, which some players may find distressing.

Even shorter game time, fewer items around, and only dangerous storms.
